title: Permutation In String
description:
Permutation In String

Given two strings s1 and s2, return true if s2 contains a permutation of s1, or false otherwise.

In other words, return true if one of s1's permutations is the substring of s2.

Examples:

  • Input: s1 = "ab", s2 = "eidbaooo"
    Output: true
    Explanation: s2 contains one permutation of s1: "ba".
  • Input: s1 = "ab", s2 = "eidboaoo"
    Output: false

Constraints:

  • 1 <= s1.length, s2.length <= 104
  • s1 and s2 consist of lowercase English letters.
solution_text:
Permutation In String

Algorithm (Sliding Window with Frequency Matching):

  1. Count character frequencies of s1.
  2. Use a sliding window of size len(s1) over s2.
  3. Track how many character frequencies match between the window and s1.
  4. When all 26 characters match, we found a permutation.
  5. Slide the window: add the new right character, remove the old left character, update match count.

Time Complexity: O(n) where n = len(s2)

Space Complexity: O(1) — fixed 26-character arrays
verify:
def verify(actual_output, expected_output):
    expected_bool = expected_output == 'true'
    passed = actual_output == expected_bool
    return [passed, str(actual_output)]
